case.title: Issue #12: Expose structural risk explanations for topology analyses
case.description: Create a user-facing explanation layer for structural topology analyses. Done means cycles, components, bridges, cutpoints, and fragility can be explained in CaseGraph work language while JSON keeps machine-readable evidence such as node ids, edge pairs, separated components, projection metadata, and raw fields where useful. The implementation must stay read-only, must not mutate case state, and must not create a new source-of-truth surface. Inputs: GitHub issue #12 and the topology substrate stabilized by issue #9. Out of scope: new topology projections, temporal topology, higher-order topology, and a stable raw topology CLI.

node.title: Expose structural risk explanations without changing source of truth
node.description: Done means users can read cycles, disconnected components, bridges, cutpoints, and fragility as work-oriented risks: dependency loops, disconnected unresolved work regions, single-point bridge dependencies, cutpoint tasks, and prioritized intervention candidates. JSON output must preserve enough evidence for automation: node ids, edge pairs, projection metadata, separated components or affected regions, and raw topology fields where appropriate. Quality constraint: choose the smallest read-only implementation surface over the stabilized issue #9 topology substrate and prove it with bug-finding verification before closure.

node.title: Choose quality-first explanation-layer shape
node.description: Decision needed before implementation: select the approach with the lowest bug risk. Preferred direction is a thin read-only explanation layer that consumes existing structural analysis results rather than recomputing topology or widening projection semantics. Rejected direction is a broad rewrite that mixes new projections, raw topology promotion, CLI UX redesign, and explanation wording in one change.

metadata.decision_result: Use a thin read-only explanation layer over existing structural analysis outputs. Freeze the explanation contract first, implement the minimum helper or CLI adaptation needed, then use adversarial fixtures and acceptance gates to find mismatches. Do not introduce new projections, do not mutate case state, and do not promote raw topology as the primary user vocabulary.

node.title: Freeze the structural explanation contract before code changes
node.description: Capture the exact mapping from structural analysis results to user-facing CaseGraph language before implementation. The contract must cover beta_0/components as disconnected unresolved work regions, beta_1/cycle witnesses as dependency loops or mutual blocking structures, bridges as single-point dependency edges, cutpoints as single-point tasks, and fragility as prioritized intervention candidates. It must also define JSON evidence fields, projection metadata, warning propagation, and the read-only/no-new-source-of-truth boundary. Artifacts likely touched: docs/spec/05-cli.md, docs/README.md, docs/README.ja.md, or a focused ADR/spec note if needed. Proof before done: evidence containing the contract matrix, rejected broader semantics, and the smallest implementation surface allowed.

node.title: Implement the smallest read-only explanation layer
node.description: Apply the frozen contract with the minimum implementation that avoids duplicate topology logic. Prefer consuming existing cycles/components/bridges/cutpoints/fragility result data and adding explanation summaries or fields at the presentation boundary. Do not recompute topology independently, do not widen projection semantics, and do not introduce state mutation. Artifacts likely touched: packages/kernel/src/analysis-*.ts only if shared typed explanation data belongs in the kernel, packages/cli/src/app.ts or result formatting only if the CLI presentation is the right boundary, and focused tests. Proof before done: targeted tests for the changed surface plus checkpoint evidence listing touched files, unchanged boundaries, and residual risks.

node.title: Find explanation bugs with adversarial fixtures
node.description: Add verification designed to discover bugs rather than only confirm the happy path. Cover release-style cases plus adversarial structures: empty scope warnings, self-loop ignored warnings, duplicate hard edges, disconnected forests, simple and figure-eight loops, bridge removal meaning, cutpoint separation meaning, and fragility ranking ties or evidence ambiguity. Tests should assert both human-oriented claims and machine-readable evidence fields. Artifacts likely touched: tests/analysis-structure.test.ts, tests/analysis-golden.test.ts, tests/fixtures/*, and tests/helpers/analysis-golden.ts or analysis-eval helpers if representative golden/eval checks are needed. Proof before done: failing-first or bug-finding notes where possible, then passing targeted tests.

node.title: Run the full acceptance and non-mutation gate
node.description: Run the final gate only after implementation and bug-finding verification land. Confirm the explanation layer is read-only, does not create a new source-of-truth surface, preserves JSON evidence for automation, and keeps raw mathematical fields secondary to work-oriented language. Required proof: pnpm typecheck, pnpm build, pnpm test:analysis-golden, pnpm test:analysis-eval, targeted Vitest for structural analysis and CLI output, plus a non-mutation check such as event log stability or explicit no-write reasoning for read-only commands. Attach exact command results before marking done.

node.title: Issue #12 scope and acceptance snapshot
node.description: Issue #12 requires a user-facing explanation layer that translates raw topology and structural analysis into CaseGraph work language. Required mappings: beta_0 to disconnected unresolved work regions; beta_1 and cycle witnesses to dependency loops or mutual blocking structures; bridges and cutpoints to single points of structural dependency; fragility ranking to prioritized intervention candidates. Acceptance: CLI output or docs show examples for cycles, components, bridges, cutpoints, and fragility; JSON output includes evidence such as node ids, edge pairs, separated components, and projection metadata; tests or golden fixtures pin representative release-style output; the explanation layer is read-only and does not introduce a new source-of-truth surface.

node.title: Quality-first implementation and verification structure
node.description: User requirement captured: choose the implementation path with the fewest bugs and use verification to discover bugs. Adopted structure: 1) freeze the explanation contract before changing code, 2) implement only a thin read-only layer over existing structural analysis outputs, 3) add adversarial fixtures designed to expose explanation mismatches, 4) run full acceptance and non-mutation gates before closure. This avoids broad projection expansion and prevents raw topology vocabulary from becoming the primary user surface.

node.title: Frozen structural risk explanation contract
node.description: Updated docs/README.md, docs/README.ja.md, and docs/spec/05-cli.md to freeze the issue #12 explanation contract before code changes. The contract maps components/beta_0 to disconnected unresolved work regions, cycles/beta_1 witnesses to dependency loops or mutual blocking structures, bridges to single dependency edges that separate unresolved work regions, cutpoints to single tasks/events that separate unresolved work regions, and fragility to prioritized intervention candidates with evidence tags and metrics. JSON evidence is fixed to preserve projection metadata, node ids, edge pairs, separated node sets, raw counts, warnings, and useful metrics. The docs explicitly keep outputs read-only and reject new projections, temporal topology, higher-order topology, stable raw topology CLI, broad UX redesign, and mutation/source-of-truth changes. Verification: git diff --check passed.

node.title: Implemented read-only structural risk explanations
node.description: Added a thin read-only explanation layer over existing structural analysis outputs. New packages/kernel/src/analysis-explanations.ts defines typed explanation entries with kind, label, summary, and machine-readable evidence. cycles/components/bridges/cutpoints/fragility now return top-level explanations derived from already-computed outputs, preserving projection, goal_node_id, warnings, node ids, edge pairs, separated node sets, raw counts, and metrics. packages/cli/src/runtime.ts renders explanation summaries for structural text output while JSON remains additive. No new projection semantics, event writes, projection mappings, or state mutation were added. Verification run by worker and re-run by parent: pnpm exec vitest run tests/analysis-structure.test.ts tests/cli.test.ts passed 22/22; pnpm typecheck passed.

node.title: Bug-finding verification for structural explanations
node.description: Added adversarial verification for the issue #12 explanation layer. tests/analysis-structure.test.ts now asserts explanation evidence stays aligned with raw results for cycles, components, bridges, cutpoints, and fragility; covers simple dependency loops, noisy figure-eight structures with self-loop warning and duplicate hard-edge normalization, disconnected forests, accurate bridge/cutpoint separated node sets, and deterministic fragility ties. tests/cli.test.ts asserts JSON explanations are present and text output uses work-oriented summaries while preserving warnings. tests/helpers/analysis-golden.ts now includes explanation_evidence checks across structural golden scenarios. No real implementation bug was found; tests passed against the current read-only implementation. Verification re-run by parent: pnpm exec vitest run tests/analysis-structure.test.ts tests/cli.test.ts tests/analysis-golden.test.ts passed 27/27 with golden 111/111 and explanation_evidence 17/17; pnpm typecheck passed; pnpm lint passed.

node.title: Final acceptance and non-mutation gate passed
node.description: Final verification for issue #12 passed. Worker ran: pnpm typecheck PASS; pnpm build PASS; pnpm test:analysis-golden PASS with 111/111 exact-match checks and hit rate 1; pnpm test:analysis-eval PASS with exact 111/111, invariant 105/105, partial labels 79/79, external manifest skipped; pnpm exec vitest run tests/analysis-structure.test.ts tests/cli.test.ts PASS with 26/26 tests; pnpm test PASS with 27 files and 150 tests. Non-mutation gate used a disposable workspace and ran analyze cycles/components/bridges/cutpoints/fragility after recording events.jsonl size/hash and full case-directory hash. events size stayed 6212 bytes, events hash stayed 15bc23b9c8e8b6a002ba7200ba4222e84d4d9a0c228dd6b1705825cec53a5b11, case directory hash stayed 6d804301b728e9a7ead752c856bb1e01caacdc4c2efaf40d1ca8e4b5943a3cad, readonly_result=PASSED. Boundary review confirmed raw topology remains experimental, stable CLI remains cycles/components/bridges/cutpoints/fragility, commands are wired through runWorkspaceCommand, workspace analysis wrappers load state and return results without mutation context, and CLI text rendering is additive over JSON explanations. No product failures or fixes were found during this final gate.
